skill.name: full-stack-orchestration-full-stack-feature
skill.description: Guides AI agents through full-stack feature planning, implementation, testing, deployment, and operations.
skill.summary: Coordinate full-stack feature delivery from architecture through rollout.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, SOC2 text, and weak-crypto keyword matches, but review found no executable code, shell invocation, network access, or prompt injection. The skill is a prose orchestration checklist for AI-assisted full-stack delivery and is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Mistaken for Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings at lines 21 and 108-113 are Markdown inline code spans for a resource path and configuration names. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around plain text values. No Ruby file, shell syntax, command interpolation, or execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SOC2 Compliance Text Mistaken for C2 Indicator
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword finding is caused by the compliance example on line 113, which mentions SOC2. This is a normal compliance standard reference, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a configuration option listing GDPR, HIPAA, and SOC2. There is no network endpoint, beaconing logic, payload handling, or malware-like context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are keyword matches inside descriptive text, headings, or planning prompts. No cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found only natural-language workflow instructions. There is no code that imports, configures, or calls MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak algorithm.

content.value_statement: Full-stack features often fail when backend, frontend, testing, and deployment work are planned separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a phased workflow for coordinated delivery.
actual_capabilities: Breaks feature delivery into architecture, implementation, testing, deployment, and operations phases.
Coordinates database schema design, backend service architecture, and frontend component planning.
Defines prompts for specialized agents covering backend, frontend, database, testing, security, and deployment work.
Promotes API-first development with OpenAPI or GraphQL contracts before implementation.
Includes verification areas for contract tests, end-to-end tests, security review, observability, and performance.
limitations: It provides orchestration guidance, not executable source code by itself.
It assumes compatible subagents or equivalent specialists are available.
It requires project-specific requirements, stack choices, and deployment constraints from the user.
It does not replace manual review of security, compliance, or production rollout decisions.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a feature idea into coordinated database, API, frontend, test, and deployment workstreams.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Specialist Agent Work
use_cases.description: Assign clear prompts and expected outputs to backend, frontend, database, testing, security, and deployment agents.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Production Rollout
use_cases.description: Check feature flags, observability, performance, security, CI/CD gates, and rollback planning before release.

prompt_templates.title: Start Feature Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a simple full-stack feature plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a full-stack feature for: [feature]. Include database, backend, frontend, testing, and deployment steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create API-First Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an API-first architecture for: [feature]. Focus on data models, service boundaries, API contracts, and frontend consumption.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Parallel Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Split work across specialists after architecture is approved.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to coordinate implementation for: [feature]. Produce backend, frontend, database, and testing workstreams with dependencies and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Release Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Validate a feature before production rollout.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review release readiness for: [feature]. Cover contract tests, E2E tests, security, observability, performance, feature flags, and rollback.

output_examples.input: Plan a customer billing dashboard with export support.
output_examples.output: A phased delivery plan covering billing data models, dashboard APIs, React components, contract tests, E2E journeys, monitoring, and rollout controls.

output_examples.input: Coordinate implementation for a GraphQL comments feature.
output_examples.output: Separate backend, frontend, database, testing, and deployment workstreams with required context, expected deliverables, and integration checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Review readiness for a new onboarding flow.
output_examples.output: A release checklist covering API validation, accessibility, security risks, feature flags, dashboards, performance targets, and rollback procedures.

best_practices: Provide clear feature requirements, target stack, deployment target, and compliance constraints before starting.
Review architecture outputs before asking implementation agents to build code.
Keep API contracts, tests, observability, and rollout controls traceable to the original requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not skip architecture and ask all agents to implement independently.
Do not treat generated deployment or security guidance as approved production policy.
Do not use vague feature descriptions when database and API contracts must be precise.

faq.question: Does this skill write the full application code?
faq.answer: No. It structures the work and prompts specialized agents or developers to produce implementation outputs.

faq.question: Which stacks does it support?
faq.answer: It is stack-flexible and includes examples such as React, Next.js, FastAPI, Django, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB.

faq.question: Can it support REST and GraphQL?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks the user to choose REST or GraphQL during API design.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers contract tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests, visual regression, and load testing scenarios.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains prose instructions only and no executable code, network calls, or command execution.

faq.question: What inputs should users provide?
faq.answer: Users should provide the feature goal, technology stack, deployment target, API style, testing depth, and compliance needs.
